question: Given a sorted array of integers and a target value, find two numbers such that they add up to the target. Return their 1-based indices. You can assume exactly one solution exists.

reference_solutions.c:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int n,target;scanf("%d %d",&n,&target);int arr[n];for(int i=0;i<n;i++)scanf("%d",&arr[i]);int l=0,r=n-1;while(l<r){int s=arr[l]+arr[r];if(s==target){printf("%d %d",l+1,r+1);break;}else if(s<target)l++;else r--;}}
reference_solutions.java: import java.util.*; class Main{public static void main(String[] args){Scanner sc=new Scanner(System.in);int n=sc.nextInt(),target=sc.nextInt();int[] arr=new int[n];for(int i=0;i<n;i++)arr[i]=sc.nextInt();int l=0,r=n-1;while(l<r){int s=arr[l]+arr[r];if(s==target){System.out.println((l+1)+" "+(r+1));break;}else if(s<target)l++;else r--;}}}
reference_solutions.javascript: const input=require('fs').readFileSync(0,'utf-8').trim().split(/\s+/).map(Number);let n=input[0],target=input[1];let arr=input.slice(2);let l=0,r=n-1;while(l<r){let s=arr[l]+arr[r];if(s===target){console.log(l+1,r+1);break;}else if(s<target)l++;else r--;}
